setting.time: a quiet night, long after bedtime
setting.place: the star-speckled sky above a small, sleeping village

characters.name: Luna
characters.motivation: Luna is tired from shining every single night and longs to rest, but she's afraid the world below will be left in the dark
characters.arc: Luna learns that asking for help is not a weakness, and that even the sky deserves to sleep

characters.name: Pip
characters.motivation: Pip wants to prove that even the smallest light matters
characters.arc: Pip discovers that gathering friends together creates something bigger than any one glow

characters.name: The Village Children
characters.role: supporting — the ones who notice Luna is fading
characters.motivation: They want to make sure their friend the moon is okay
characters.arc: They learn to care for the things that watch over them

acts.name: The Yawning Sky
acts.summary: Luna tries to keep shining but can't stop yawning. Stars blink in concern as her glow dims to a soft flicker.
scenes: Opening: Luna rises over the village hills but keeps yawning great golden yawns that make her light wobble
The stars ask Luna what's wrong, and she admits she hasn't slept in a very long time — hundreds and hundreds of nights
Luna's glow fades to the faintest pearl shimmer, and the village below grows darker than it has ever been

acts.name: The Tiny Volunteer
acts.summary: A firefly named Pip notices the dark sky and rallies the night creatures to keep watch while Luna rests.
scenes: Pip zips up to the clouds and finds Luna curled up, her eyes heavy and drooping
Pip promises Luna that the night creatures will hold the light, and gently covers Luna with a blanket of thin silver clouds
Luna gives one last sleepy smile and drifts off, her breathing becoming the soft tide of the night

acts.name: A Thousand Little Lights
acts.summary: Pip gathers fireflies, glowworms, and even bioluminescent mushrooms to light the village in Luna's absence.
scenes: Pip flies from garden to garden, pond to pond, calling every glowing creature to rise together
The village children look up from their windows and see a swarm of tiny lights drifting across the sky like living stars
The children whisper 'thank you' to the fireflies and tuck themselves back into bed, feeling safe

acts.name: Morning, and a Well-Rested Moon
acts.summary: Dawn arrives. Luna wakes refreshed and brighter than ever, grateful and glowing with a new warmth.
scenes: Pip lands gently on Luna's edge as the first pink light of dawn peeks over the hills
Luna stretches and rises, shining with a silvery glow that seems to laugh, thanking every little light by name
From that night on, Luna sleeps one small nap every week, and the night creatures take turns keeping watch — a promise between the sky and the smallest lights below
